fr:frontmatter.fr:title: Aspects of predicative algebraic set theory III: sheaves
fr:frontmatter.fr:meta: Proceedings of the London Mathematical Society

@article{van-den-berg-moerdijk-2012, author = {{van den Berg}, Benno and Moerdijk, Ieke}, url = {https://londmathsoc.onlinelibrary.wiley.com/doi/abs/10.1112/plms/pdr066}, year = {2012}, doi = {10.1112/plms/pdr066}, eprint = {https://londmathsoc.onlinelibrary.wiley.com/doi/pdf/10.1112/plms/pdr066}, journal = {Proceedings of the London Mathematical Society}, number = {5}, pages = {1076--1122}, title = {Aspects of predicative algebraic set theory III: sheaves}, volume = {105}, }

fr:mainmatter.fr:p: This is the third instalment in a series of papers on algebraic set theory. In it, we develop a uniform approach to sheaf models of constructive set theories based on ideas from categorical logic. The key notion is that of a ‘predicative category with small maps’ which axiomatizes the idea of a category of classes and class morphisms, together with a selected class of maps whose fibres are sets (in some axiomatic set theory). The main result of the present paper is that such predicative categories with small maps are stable under internal sheaves. We discuss the sheaf models of constructive set theory this leads to, as well as ideas for future work.

fr:meta.name: venue
fr:meta.fr:link: Yorkshire and Midlands Category Theory Seminar #32

fr:mainmatter.fr:p: Kristina Sojakova described a huge advance on her prior work on syllepsis in homotopy type theory. In the prior work, there is a lot of path algebra to establish the syllepsis — to the point where going any further is practically impossible. Kristina has come up with a new way to prove Eckmann–Hilton, syllepsis, and higher syllepses that completely avoids this path algebra.

The idea is to generalize each statement to get rid of all the loops; in other words, a loop x : A, p: x = x is replaced by x : A, y : A, p : x = y as well as whatever other things are needed in order to generalize the statement involving loops. This doesn’t appear to be completely mechanical, but it is at least methodical. Once all the loops have been removed, the generalized statement is proved by applying path induction a ton of times, mindlessly. Then the original statement involving loops is easily seen to be an instance of the generalized one.
The new argument scales effortlessly from Eckmann–Hilton to syllepsis, to the “syllepsis of the syllepsis”.

This was easily the most interesting talk of YaMCATS#32 for me; I am wondering if there are more painful things in HoTT that can be dealt with in this way. Jamie Vicary asked whether the proof is depending heavily on path induction having definitional computation rules; I think this is indeed the crux of the proof, although Benno van den Berg mentioned that it might still be possible to use this proof technique with the weak version of path induction. I think this is within the realm of “possibility” but I think it would be very painful.

fr:frontmatter.fr:title: IAS Special Year on Univalent Foundations

fr:mainmatter.fr:p: A Special Year on Univalent Foundations of Mathematics was held in 2012-13 at the Institute for Advanced Study, School of Mathematics, organized by Steve Awodey, Thierry Coquand, and Vladimir Voevodsky. The following people were the official participants:
There were also the following students, whose participation was no less valuable.
In addition, there were the following short- and long-term visitors, including student visitors, whose contributions to the Special Year were also essential.
